changes_summary: Expanded scope to address all 13 open flags: (1) added useTimelineTrackManagement.ts as a full step covering moveClipToRow, createTrackAndMoveClip, keyboard moves, and batch moves; (2) moved interactionStateRef ownership from TimelineEditor.tsx up into useTimelineState.ts so useTimelineSave/persistence/poll can consume it; (3) committed to the preserve-don't-rebuild auto-sync policy in usePinnedShotGroups with rationale; (4) specified how the clip session is extended with pairClipId/pairBoundary fields to absorb the group-child-boundary session without losing two-clip context; (5) enumerated all three useClipDrag caller branches and made the new-track branch a pure rows+trackId update with no pinnedShotGroupsOverride; (6) added rowsToConfig/clonePinnedShotGroups in timeline-data.ts plus render-bounds.validation.test.ts to the shape-strip pass; (7) enumerated the broader test surface (useTimelineTrackManagement, useExternalDrop, useClipEditing, useClipDrag, render-bounds.validation, serialize, timeline-save-utils); (8) flagged the geometry-bearing callers in usePinnedShotGroups and useSwitchToFinalVideo for signature changes. Restructured into 5 phases / 15 steps. No scope creep beyond the rollback — all additions are in the video-editor tool and trace back to projection-era code paths.

success_criteria.criterion: Loading a config with legacy `pinnedShotGroups[].children` yields runtime groups in the new soft-tag shape, and saving that same timeline emits no `start` or `children` fields.

No runtime load/save/commit path re-derives clip timing or track placement from shot-group child geometry; `clips[]` remains the only source of truth for `at` and duration. This includes `rowsToConfig()`, `clonePinnedShotGroups()`, and `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry`.
Dragging the right edge of the last clip in a shot changes only that clip's duration and the group's rendered right boundary follows.
Dragging the left edge of the first clip in a shot changes only that clip's start/duration and the group's rendered left boundary follows.

Dragging an interior boundary inside a shot changes only the two adjacent clips while preserving the rest of the group, and the collapsed `clip` resize session carries both clip ids and clamps without regressing the previous paired-preview behavior.

Dragging a grouped clip body translates the full group together. Cross-track drag — including the create-new-track branch — moves all grouped clips via `type: 'rows'` and updates the group's `trackId`, with no `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` config branch remaining.

Grouped cross-track moves initiated from `useTimelineTrackManagement` (drag, keyboard move-selected, create-track-and-move) all route through the soft-tag model with no calls to `moveGroup()` or `type: 'pinnedShotGroups'` for timing.

`interactionStateRef` is created in `useTimelineState.ts` and shared with `useTimelineSave`, `useTimelinePersistence`, `usePollSync`, `useClipDrag`, and `useClipResize`; auto-save does not execute during an active drag or resize, and the newest pending save flushes after the interaction ends.

Poll-sync and conflict reloads do not overwrite local edits during an active drag or resize interaction; only the newest deferred remote payload is re-evaluated against current local state after the interaction ends.

`usePinnedShotGroups` auto-sync preserves live timing of surviving member clips: added images append at the group right edge, removed images delete only their member clip, and no trailing shift is applied.
Shot label menu actions and per-clip context-menu lockdown still work under the soft-tag model.

The video-editor test suite passes after updating intentional expectations across migrate, serialize, timeline-save-utils, render-bounds.validation, useTimelineTrackManagement, useExternalDrop, useClipEditing, useClipDrag, useShotGroups, usePollSync, and persistence tests, with no new failures beyond the known unrelated pre-existing TimelineCanvas assertion if it still remains.
Net LOC or helper surface across shot-group-specific files decreases after removing projection machinery.
An unchanged timeline produces the same clip-level data for Remotion preview before and after the refactor.
Manual browser smoke testing confirms the two reported resize/save-reset symptoms no longer reproduce.

assumptions: The brief's rollback direction is correct: a structural removal of projection is simpler and safer than incremental fixes because projection is wired into load, save, commit, drag, resize, track management, and rendering.
`(shotId, trackId)` remains the identity for a pinned group; no new stable group id is introduced.
Stored `clipIds` is ordered left-to-right by live clip `at` and is rebuilt whenever membership or position changes.

Auto-sync of pinned groups against the underlying shot preserves live timing of surviving members rather than rebuilding the sequence (committed in Step 11 to avoid re-introducing the silent-revert bug class on a different axis).
When save or poll work is deferred during an interaction, only the newest pending item is kept and re-evaluated against current local state once the interaction clears.

The collapsed `clip` resize session is allowed to carry an optional `pairClipId`/`pairBoundary` so interior-boundary trades retain the two-clip context the old `group-child-boundary` session relied on.
Long-lived diagnostics outside the shot-group and timeline-sync paths can stay; only temporary debugging logs added during this bug cycle are removed.
